data.title: Webuild Publishes Updated Bylaws, Underscoring Governance Transparency
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Webuild SpA ( (IT:WBD) ) has provided an announcement.

Webuild S.p.A., the Italian-based infrastructure group recognized as a global leader in sustainable mobility, hydroelectric and water infrastructure, and green building, continues to leverage its 120-year track record across nearly 50 countries, supported by a €58.4 billion order backlog that is largely aligned with UN Sustainable Development Goals. Listed in Milan and included in the MIB ESG index, the company’s extensive portfolio spans flagship projects such as the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam, the Panama Canal expansion, and key metro, rail, and highway corridors in Europe, the Americas, the Middle East, and Australia.

The company announced that its newly adopted corporate bylaws have been filed with the Companies Register of Milan – Monza Brianza – Lodi and are now publicly available through its website, registered office, and the 1Info authorized storage system. The publication of the updated bylaws signals a completed step in Webuild’s governance evolution and provides investors and other stakeholders with enhanced transparency on the group’s corporate framework, potentially reinforcing its positioning within ESG-focused capital markets and among global infrastructure partners.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:WBD) stock is a Buy with a EUR3.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Webuild SpA stock, see the IT:WBD Stock Forecast page.

More about Webuild SpA

Webuild S.p.A. is a global leader in large and complex infrastructure construction, focusing on sustainable mobility, hydroelectric power, water management, and green building, and is a long-standing top player worldwide in water-sector infrastructure. Active in about 50 countries with roughly 95,000 people and more than 3,700 completed projects, it is listed on the Milan Stock Exchange and features in the MIB ESG index for its strong environmental, social, and governance profile.

Headquartered in Italy and directed by Salini Costruttori S.p.A., Webuild has built railways, metro lines, roads, bridges, tunnels, dams, and hydro plants, including landmark works such as the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam, the Panama Canal expansion, and major metro systems in European and Middle Eastern capitals. As of 31 December 2025, it reported €13.6 billion in revenues and a €58.4 billion order backlog, with over 95% of construction activities aligned with United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, underscoring its strategic focus on sustainable infrastructure worldwide.

Average Trading Volume: 3,665,044

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: €2.6B

Find detailed analytics on WBD stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: UBS Warns Swiss Capital Plan Could Add $37 Billion Burden After Credit Suisse Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B ( (AMUB) ) has issued an announcement.

On 22 April 2026, UBS sharply criticized new Swiss regulatory capital measures after the Federal Council finalized changes to the Capital Adequacy Ordinance and sent proposed Banking Act amendments on foreign participations to parliament, arguing the package is extreme, not internationally aligned and potentially damaging to the Swiss economy. The revised rules will tighten capital treatment for capitalized software and prudential valuation adjustments from 2027–2029, cutting about USD 4 billion of CET1 at group level and USD 2 billion at UBS AG standalone, while a separate proposal to fully deduct foreign participations from CET1 over seven years would force UBS AG to hold roughly USD 20 billion in additional CET1, lifting total extra capital needs linked to the Credit Suisse deal to about USD 37 billion and driving annual capital costs of around USD 3 billion.

UBS said the Swiss government’s pro‑forma capital ratios and peer comparisons are misleading and warned that the combined impact of the measures would push its de facto minimum group CET1 ratio to about 18.4%, or 17.6% after CAO-related derecognition, further understating its capital strength versus rivals. Citing an independent BAK Economics study forecasting up to CHF 34 billion in lost Swiss GDP over ten years from the foreign participation rules, UBS questioned the government’s impact assessment but stressed it will maintain its 2026 financial targets and planned capital returns, remain committed to its diversified business model, and seek ways to protect shareholders while limiting adverse effects on clients and employees as the parliamentary process unfolds.

More about ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B

UBS Group AG, headquartered in Zurich and Basel, is a global financial services provider and Switzerland’s largest bank, with a core focus on wealth management, domestic Swiss banking, asset management and a targeted investment banking franchise. Following its acquisition of Credit Suisse, UBS operates as a systemically important institution with a substantial international footprint and is subject to some of the world’s most stringent capital regulations.

Average Trading Volume: 612

See more data about AMUB stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Protector Forsikring Delivers Strong Premium Growth but Weaker Profits on Investment Losses in Q1 2026
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Protector Forsikring ASA ( (PSKRF) ) has issued an update.

Protector Forsikring ASA reported a strong top-line performance in the first quarter of 2026, with gross written premiums up 20% year-on-year, or 21% in local currencies, and a solid combined ratio of 84.9% indicating continued underwriting profitability. Growth was supported by key renewal dates, including a NOK 122m increase in UK gross written premiums on 1 April, underscoring the insurer’s strategic push in that market.

Despite the robust underwriting, results were hit by weaker investment performance, with total investment return including insurance finance at negative NOK 231m, contributing to a sharp drop in profit to NOK 165m and earnings per share of NOK 1.8. Even so, the company maintained a strong post-dividend solvency ratio of 220% and the board approved a dividend of NOK 659m, or NOK 8.00 per share, signaling confidence in capital strength and ongoing shareholder payouts.

The most recent analyst rating on (PSKRF) stock is a Hold with a NOK544.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Protector Forsikring ASA stock, see the PSKRF Stock Forecast page.

More about Protector Forsikring ASA

Protector Forsikring ASA is a Nordic insurance company focused on non-life insurance, offering products such as commercial and public sector property and casualty cover. The group has a growing presence in the UK market, aligning its premium inflows with key inception dates in both the Nordic region and the UK to drive international expansion.

Average Trading Volume: 220,765

Current Market Cap: NOK40.44B

For a thorough assessment of PSKRF stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Mips CFO Karin Rosenthal to Step Down After Six Years in Role
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from MIPS AB ( (SE:MIPS) ) is now available.

Mips AB, the Stockholm-listed safety technology group behind the Mips and Koroyd ingredient brands, develops science-based impact protection systems that are integrated into helmets and other protective products by major global manufacturers. Its technologies draw on more than 30 years of research and advanced aerospace safety science, reinforcing the company’s role as a specialist supplier in the premium protective equipment market.

The company announced that Chief Financial Officer Karin Rosenthal, who has held the role since 2020, will step down from her position on 22 October 2026 following a mutually agreed decision with the board. She will remain in post to support the immediate search for a successor and ensure an orderly handover, a transition the CEO framed as occurring as Mips enters a new phase of development, suggesting continuity in financial leadership during a strategically important period for the group and its stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (SE:MIPS) stock is a Buy with a SEK540.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on MIPS AB stock, see the SE:MIPS Stock Forecast page.

More about MIPS AB

Mips Group is a global safety company that operates an ingredient-brand business model through its Koroyd and Mips brands, supplying advanced impact protection technologies to leading helmet and protective gear manufacturers worldwide. Headquartered in Stockholm and listed on Nasdaq Stockholm, the group leverages decades of research partnerships, including with the Swedish Royal Institute of Technology and Karolinska Institutet, to develop innovative helmet-based and aerospace-derived safety solutions.

Average Trading Volume: 125,101

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: SEK7.24B

An announcement from Fitness Champs Holdings Limited ( (FCHL) ) is now available.

Fitness Champs Holdings Limited, a leading Singapore aquatic sports education company best known for its SwimSafer school programs and private “Fitness Champs” lessons since 2012, aims to make swimming accessible and affordable while broadening into diversified sports education. The group’s focus on water safety, fitness and new offerings such as pickleball positions it to tap growing demand for structured recreational sports in its home market.

On April 17, 2026, Fitness Champs priced a best-efforts U.S. public offering of 3,225,000 units at $1.55 each, raising about $5 million in gross proceeds, and closed the transaction on April 20, 2026. The mix of shares, pre-funded warrants and short-dated warrants, combined with 90-day lock-ups for key insiders, gives the company fresh capital for research development, business expansion and working capital, while potentially increasing its U.S. shareholder base and funding its push into a broader sports education platform.

Spark’s Take on FCHL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FCHL is a Underperform.

The score is primarily pressured by weak financial performance quality (margin deterioration, weakened cash generation, and a sharply higher leverage profile with very thin equity). Technicals add further caution with negative momentum and a broader downtrend. Valuation offers limited support due to a negative P/E and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on FCHL stock, click here.

More about Fitness Champs Holdings Limited

Fitness Champs Holdings Limited is a Singapore-based aquatic sports education provider offering general swimming lessons for children and adults, including ladies-only classes and aquatic sports such as water polo, competitive swimming and lifesaving. It is one of the largest providers of school-based swimming lessons under Singapore’s Ministry of Education SwimSafer program and plans to expand into broader sports education, including new sports like pickleball.

data.title: Connexa Investors Approve Key Governance and Capital Measures
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Connexa Sports Technologies ( (YYAI) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 17, 2026, AiRWA Inc. held its 2026 annual meeting of stockholders, with 19,689,058 of 42,142,432 eligible common shares represented, establishing a quorum for voting. Shareholders elected five directors to serve until the next annual meeting, ratified Enrome LLP as independent auditor for the fiscal year ending April 30, 2026, and approved executive compensation along with holding future say-on-pay votes every three years.

Investors also approved an amendment to the 2026 AiRWA Share Incentive Plan to raise available shares to 3,500,000 and add an evergreen provision allowing annual share pool increases through 2030, and authorized the board to execute one or more reverse stock splits at ratios between 1-for-40 and 1-for-800 over the next two years. These decisions collectively enhance the company’s flexibility in managing equity compensation and capital structure, reinforcing board discretion over dilution, share count, and governance of executive pay.

Spark’s Take on YYAI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, YYAI is a Underperform.

YYAI’s overall stock score reflects significant financial and operational challenges. Declining revenues, negative profitability, and high liabilities weigh heavily. Technical indicators show a bearish trend, and valuation metrics are poor, with the recent earnings call adding further uncertainty.

To see Spark’s full report on YYAI stock, click here.

More about Connexa Sports Technologies

AiRWA Inc. is a publicly traded company whose common stock is listed and held by a broad base of stockholders. The company operates under a conventional U.S. corporate governance framework, using annual meetings and proxy proposals to manage board composition, executive compensation, equity incentive plans, and capital structure decisions affecting its common shareholders.

Cranswick ( (GB:CWK) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Cranswick plc disclosed that director Christopher Aldersley has acquired a small number of ordinary shares under the company’s 2024 Buy As You Earn Share Incentive Plan. The purchase, executed on 21 April 2026 via the London Stock Exchange, forms part of a regular monthly partnership share purchase programme and signals continued managerial participation in the group’s employee share ownership scheme.

The transaction involves the acquisition of three ordinary shares at a price of 5,400 pence per share, in line with the structure of the BAYE plan. While modest in size, such routine director dealings underscore ongoing alignment between executive interests and those of shareholders, and reflect Cranswick’s continued use of share-based incentives to support long-term engagement and retention of senior management.

data.description:

Lloyds Banking ( (GB:LLOY) ) has provided an update.

Lloyds Banking Group has repurchased 5,000,000 of its ordinary shares on 22 April 2026 as part of its existing share buyback programme, using Goldman Sachs International as broker at a volume-weighted average price of 100.5037 pence per share. The bank intends to cancel these shares, a move that will reduce its share count and can enhance earnings per share, signaling continued capital strength and an ongoing commitment to returning surplus capital to shareholders.

This transaction follows instructions issued to the broker in late January 2026, indicating a planned, programmatic approach to capital management rather than an ad hoc intervention. By continuing to execute and cancel shares under the buyback, Lloyds reinforces its capital allocation strategy and may support its share price and investor confidence in a competitive U.K. banking sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:LLOY) stock is a Buy with a £120.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Lloyds Banking stock, see the GB:LLOY Stock Forecast page.

The score is driven primarily by a strong, upgrade-supported earnings outlook and capital return plan from the latest call. This is tempered by weaker underlying financial quality signals (higher leverage and negative free cash flow in the last two years). Technically the trend is positive, but overbought indicators add near-term risk, while valuation and yield are supportive but not standout.

data.title: BCE Announces Preferred Share Conversion Results and New Dividend Terms
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BCE ( (TSE:BCE) ) has provided an update.

BCE Inc. reported on April 21, 2026 the results of shareholder elections to convert between its fixed-rate Series AG and floating-rate Series AH preferred shares, effective May 1, 2026. After these conversions, the company will have 9,375,684 Series AG shares and 2,832,114 Series AH shares outstanding, both continuing to trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

For the five-year period starting May 1, 2026, Series AG preferred shares will pay a fixed quarterly dividend at an annual rate of 5.30%, while Series AH will maintain a monthly floating dividend tied to the prime rate and an adjustment factor linked to market pricing. The announcement clarifies BCE’s future preferred share capital structure and dividend terms, providing income-oriented investors with transparent options between fixed and floating yield exposure.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:BCE) stock is a Buy with a C$44.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BCE stock, see the TSE:BCE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BCE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BCE is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by a stable-but-levered financial profile (steady cash generation but high debt and recent earnings/FCF volatility). Valuation is a major positive (very low P/E and high yield), while technicals are modestly supportive. Earnings-call guidance is constructive on revenue/EBITDA/FCF, but tempered by expected EPS decline and competitive/media headwinds.

To see Spark’s full report on BCE stock, click here.

More about BCE

BCE Inc. is Canada’s largest communications company, providing advanced fibre and wireless networks, enterprise services and digital media to consumers and businesses. The company focuses on next-generation, cloud-based and AI-driven connectivity solutions that keep customers connected, informed and entertained while supporting corporate clients’ competitiveness globally.
